channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: AFL enforces draft rule changes despite club opposition over father-son and academy selections
item.description: Carlton and Port Adelaide have failed in their attempt to delay significant AFL draft rule changes, with the league confirming a new system aimed at making clubs pay a fairer price for elite talent ti...

item.title: Trump Rejects Iran’s Staged Peace Plan Amid Leadership Uncertainty
item.description: Efforts to resolve the ongoing conflict between the US, Israel, and Iran remain stalled as of April 28, 2026. Iranian leadership is reportedly in transition following the death of Ayatollah Ali Khamen...

item.title: PM Modi Encourages Maximum Voter Participation in West Bengal Phase-2 Elections
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi urged citizens to participate enthusiastically in the second phase of the West Bengal Assembly Elections 2026, which began on Wednesday across 142 constituencies. In a pos...

item.title: B.C. Court Upholds Criminal Conviction of Wet'suwet'en Chief Despite Indigenous Law Argument
item.description: The B.C. Court of Appeal has ruled that Wet'suwet'en Chief Dsta’hyl, also known as Adam Bernard Gagnon, cannot rely on Indigenous law to overturn his criminal conviction for contempt of court. The con...

item.title: King Charles Addresses US Congress and Meets Trump Amid Tense Diplomatic Relations
item.description: During a four-day state visit to the United States, King Charles became only the second British sovereign to address the US Congress, highlighting the enduring alliance between the UK and the US. In h...

item.title: Phase 2 of West Bengal Assembly Elections Underway with 32 Million Voters
item.description: Today, roughly 32.1 million voters in West Bengal are casting their votes in the second phase of the 2026 assembly elections. This phase covers 142 assembly constituencies across seven districts, most...

item.title: Gina Carano Reflects on Disney Settlement and Prepares for MMA Return Against Ronda Rousey
item.description: Gina Carano, former 'Mandalorian' star, has reached a legal settlement with Disney and is preparing for a high-profile MMA return against Ronda Rousey on May 16, 2026. In a recent interview with Evie ...

item.title: Nigerian Army Repels ISWAP Attack Along Maiduguri–Damaturu Road In Yobe
item.description: Troops of the Nigerian Army don show serious ginger as dem foil one attack wey suspected ISWAP terrorists try pull for Yobe State. The incident happen for early hours of April 26 near Kukareta, Damatu...

item.title: Vancouver police deny motorcade escort for FIFA president during annual meeting
item.description: The Vancouver Police Department has refused a request to provide a motorcade escort for FIFA president Gianni Infantino during the upcoming 76th FIFA Congress in Vancouver. Constable Darren Wong expla...

item.title: Filipino Families See Slight Drop in Self-Rated Poverty Before Global Oil Price Hike
item.description: A recent survey conducted by OCTA Research, known as the Tugon ng Masa (TNM) survey, reported a slight decline in self-rated poverty among Filipino families. The survey, covering March 19 to 25, 2025,...

item.title: AI-generated images falsely link accused US press gala gunman to celebrities and public figures
item.description: Following the attempted assassination of former US President Donald Trump at a Washington press gala, social media has been flooded with AI-generated images falsely connecting the accused, Cole Tomas ...

item.title: West Bengal Police Arrests 1,543 Persons Ahead of Second Phase of Assembly Elections
item.description: In West Bengal, the police have arrested a total of 1,543 individuals ahead of the second phase of the Assembly elections, including a Trinamool Congress councillor. Officials stated that these action...

item.title: Baringo Woman Appeals for Prosthetic Arm After Losing Both Hands in Fire
item.description: A woman from Baringo, Gladys Chepyegon, has left many Kenyans deeply moved after sharing her painful story on live television. Two weeks after giving birth, Gladys lost both her hands in a fire at her...

item.title: PSG edge Bayern 5-4 in high-scoring Champions League semi-final first leg
item.description: Last night for Paris, PSG make history as dem beat Bayern Munich 5-4 for UEFA Champions League semi-final first leg. The game na proper thriller with nine goals, record for this stage of the competiti...

item.title: Unions caution that temporary fuel levy relief may not prevent steep price hikes
item.description: Trade unions in South Africa have raised concerns that the government’s temporary fuel levy relief will not be enough to protect workers and the economy from upcoming fuel price increases. The relief,...

item.title: Microsoft updates digital maps to reflect Palestinian place names in West Bank
item.description: Microsoft has recently updated its digital mapping services, including Bing, to remove Israeli labels previously used for locations in the West Bank. The controversial term 'Judea and Samaria', common...

item.title: Pilot NHS programme offers earlier support to women after miscarriage
item.description: A pilot project at Birmingham Women and Children's Hospital in England aims to offer early support to women who have suffered miscarriages, potentially preventing thousands each year. Traditionally, N...

item.title: USDA moves to ease soaring fertilizer costs amid global supply disruptions and market concerns
item.description: The U.S. Department of Agriculture is taking a series of steps to address sharply rising fertilizer prices that are squeezing American farmers during the spring planting season. Agriculture Secretary ...

item.title: French River Residents Face Ongoing Flood Risks After Heavy Rain
item.description: Following recent heavy rainfall in Northern Ontario, residents of the French River area are confronting ongoing flooding concerns. Municipal authorities have reported continued road closures and disru...

item.title: UAE Leaves OPEC Amid Growing Tensions With Saudi Arabia, Signaling Gulf Policy Shift
item.description: The United Arab Emirates announced its decision to leave OPEC and OPEC+ on April 28, 2026, effective May 1, removing one of the cartel's major oil producers. This exit accounts for roughly 12% of OPEC...
